title: Product boundary and modular-monolith architecture
normative_text: Implement Release 1 as a modular monolith with strict domain/application/adapter boundaries and isolated worker processes. Builder stops at compiled target packages, Development Capsules, and signed downstream-result ingestion. External target runtimes remain outside the repository.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Architecture tests reject adapter imports from domain, direct UI/database writes, external runtime modules, and monolithic skill-owned workflows.
repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

title: Separate canonical Harness IR and Builder Workflow IR
normative_text: Maintain two versioned aggregates with separate schemas, identities, migrations, compilers, and authorization. Workflow IR may reference Harness IR paths and product graph hashes but cannot own or rewrite harness values. Harness IR cannot contain worker, queue, retry, sandbox, or deployment state.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Schema tests reject orchestration fields in Harness IR and product-semantic fields in Workflow IR. Replay tests bind every node result to exact product and workflow hashes.
architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;Harness IR And Compilers;Workflow Runtime
repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

title: PostgreSQL event ledger, snapshots, projections, and content-addressed artifacts
normative_text: Use PostgreSQL as the authoritative event, snapshot, command-receipt, registry, and projection store. Use a SHA-256 content-addressed artifact store with filesystem adapter for development and S3-compatible adapter for production. Commit artifact references and events atomically after staged bytes verify.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Contract tests run against real PostgreSQL and both CAS adapters. Fault tests cover crash between staging and event commit, duplicate delivery, backup/restore, missing object, and projection rebuild.
architecture_components: Control Tower;Evaluation;Evidence Workspace;Genesis;Harness IR And Compilers;Repair, Authorization, Handoff;Run Governance
repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

title: Deterministic compilers, canonical serialization, and schema evolution
normative_text: Use canonical JSON serialization, content hashes, immutable compiler identities, declared IR-path dependencies, atomic artifact manifests, generated JSON Schemas, and explicit migration/upcaster registries. Identical inputs must produce byte-identical deterministic artifacts or an approved nondeterminism exception.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Golden tests run across platforms/locales. Property tests verify canonical serialization. Migration tests prove forward conversion, rollback or explicit irreversibility, and old-event replay.
architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;Harness IR And Compilers;JIT Capsule Compiler;Repair, Authorization, Handoff
repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

title: Explicit human-agent-code authority and command model
normative_text: Every command, capability, and workflow node declares one primary actor: deterministic code, bounded agent, human gate, or governed hybrid. Deterministic code validates and commits. Agents produce proposals under exact capsules. Humans own constitutional decisions, creative policy, risk, waivers, irreversible architecture, and authorization. Evaluators are isolated from generators.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Authorization matrix tests cover every command. Adversarial tests attempt agent ratification, evaluator leakage, stale grants, forged receipts, and UI bypass.
repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

title: Temporal workflow-engine adapter with deterministic test runtime
normative_text: Compile governed Workflow IR to a Temporal adapter for production scheduling. Provide an in-memory deterministic adapter for unit/integration tests. Temporal workflows coordinate; activities invoke Builder application commands. Run Ledger events, not Temporal history, remain canonical product truth.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Run the same public-seam suite against in-memory and Temporal adapters. Fault tests cover worker loss, duplicate activity completion, stale signal, timeout, engine restart, and rollback.
repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

title: Immutable evidence identity and target-specific source profiles
normative_text: Every run selects a versioned target-specific Source Profile and exact target candidate before initialization. Source bytes receive SHA-256 identity, immutable descriptors, authority/license/privacy metadata, stable specimen IDs, and an aggregate Source Lock. All evidence claims link to source spans and knowledge status.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Security/property tests cover archives and identity; scale tests cover large descriptor sets; Format 02 cannot proceed without a ratified corpus and profile. title: Provider-bounded visual inference with knowledge-status separation
normative_text: Use a provider-neutral `VisualInferencePort` with task-specific typed outputs. Run deterministic normalization/geometry first, then bounded provider parsing, independent validation, and cross-specimen induction. Store measured observations, deterministic derivations, hypotheses, human decisions, and generated proposals as distinct knowledge statuses. Provider selection remains empirical: compare at least two adapters plus the deterministic baseline on protected Format 02 cases. No provider receives canonical write authority.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Golden and adversarial cases verify coordinate fidelity, BBOX/WHY separation, temporal syntax, disagreement handling, label isolation, provider failure, and category-native quality. The accepted provider-bounded inference decision is unchanged. title: Versioned skill ecology and deterministic JIT capsule compiler
normative_text: Use four layers: Canonical Skill, Harness-local Adaptation, Composition Recipe, and ephemeral JIT Execution Capsule. A versioned capability registry and necessity test govern new skills. Deterministic code resolves versions, authority, precedence, dependencies, references, context, degradation, tools, evaluators, and capsule identity.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Tests enforce necessity decisions, exact evaluated identity, deterministic capsule bytes, context blocking, precedence conflicts, revocation impact, hidden orchestration rejection, and least privilege.
repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

title: Isolated benchmark governance and maturity receipts
normative_text: Use a layered versioned corpus with public/development/protected cases, exact subject identity, no-guidance controls, repeated fresh-context trials, isolated evaluators, multidimensional scorecards, hard gates, and maturity receipts. Separate label custody and credentials from generator/runtime access. Human governance ratifies rubrics and thresholds after Format 02 calibration.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Security tests prove label isolation. Identity tests bind evaluated artifacts. Statistical/gate tests cover repeatability, hard failures, resume, leakage revocation, and Format 02 sequence fidelity. title: Event-derived FastAPI and React Control Tower
normative_text: Expose a FastAPI command/query API and a React/TypeScript Control Tower launched or linked from Pi. Read models are idempotent event projections. UI actions submit typed commands with expected versions and render authoritative receipts. The UI stores presentation preferences only.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Contract, replay, stale-command, authorization, redaction, accessibility, performance, disconnection, and projection-rebuild tests are mandatory before acceptance. title: Deny-by-default node and implementation-task isolation
normative_text: Use deny-by-default capability grants and three policy classes: deterministic process sandbox, provider-call sandbox, and isolated implementation-task sandbox. Policies specify immutable environment/image, read-only mounts, writable staging, tools, network allowlist, ephemeral secret references, CPU/memory/time limits, output contract, logs, and disposal. The ratified profiles require deny-by-default grants, read-only evidence mounts, ephemeral credentials, explicit network allowlists, and per-node worktree or container isolation. The exact conforming platform mechanism remains an implementation choice and may not weaken those policy semantics.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Escape, traversal, secret, network, tool, label, resource exhaustion, crash, cleanup, and cross-platform conformance tests are required.
repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

title: Target compilers with frozen external product boundaries
normative_text: Maintain three versioned `CompilationTargetProfile` records with distinct source profiles, IR projections, Genesis graphs, compilers, artifact sets, evaluation gates, and certification scopes. External target compilers consume read-only versioned interface snapshots and emit schemas/specifications/fixtures/packages only. Visual Asset Editor and Delegation packages remain `UNCERTIFIED` in Release 1. Their owning repositories control runtime and shared contracts. Cross-target compatibility validates content-owned fields as immutable.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Boundary tests reject external runtime modules, cross-target semantic rewrites, missing snapshots, false certification, and nondeterministic packages. The accepted three-target decision is unchanged. It must exercise every Builder subsystem from source lock through downstream signed effectiveness evidence. Other categories and external targets remain structurally supported and `UNCERTIFIED`.
acceptance_or_enforcement: End-to-end shadow, workflow, fault, security, benchmark, capsule, handoff, and downstream receipt tests must pass without certifying other profiles.

title: No V2.1 migration without repository-local authoritative artifacts
normative_text: Classify V2.1 implementation migration and compatibility as not applicable for the current repository. Design Builder Next directly from current authority. Preserve future migration hooks for Builder Next schema evolution, but do not implement V2.1 aliases, importers, dual compilation, or regression suites.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Repository inventory and CI assert no V2.1 compatibility claim or importer without an approved baseline manifest and source hash.

title: Containerized Release 1 topology and OpenTelemetry observability
normative_text: Package API, workflow worker, deterministic worker, sandboxed agent/evaluator workers, projection worker, and Control Tower UI as separate containers/processes around shared PostgreSQL, CAS, proposed workflow engine, and OpenTelemetry collector. Environments are development, CI, shadow, and production. Configuration is versioned; secrets are external references.
acceptance_or_enforcement: CI must run image, dependency, secret, network, migration, backup/restore, telemetry, promotion, and rollback tests in an environment matching production contracts.
repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

title: Mandatory Release 1 Workflow Profiles
normative_text: Require five profiles in Release 1: 1. New harness compilation. 2. Incremental evidence refresh. 3. Benchmark regression. 4. Repair and re-certification. 5. Constrained incident hotfix. Format 02 new-harness compilation is the primary shadow and production candidate. Other profiles derive from its nodes/contracts while preserving distinct triggers, authority, budgets, and gates.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Routing, authority, checkpoint, fault, promotion, rollback, and cross-profile isolation tests are mandatory. Product authority must ratify the list.
repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

acceptance_or_enforcement: A required skill below the target's maturity threshold blocks full authorization. | A stable skill change triggers all required dependent regressions.
repository_coverage_basis: No product implementation, product schema, workflow, or production test exists in the repository; this behavior requires new implementation.

title: Verify evaluated artifact identity
normative_text: The evaluation system must bind exact source IR, skill package, adaptation, recipe, capsule, compiler, model policy, dataset, and scoring versions.
acceptance_or_enforcement: The shipped artifact hash matches the evaluated artifact hash. | A changed dependency invalidates the applicable receipt.
repository_coverage_basis: No product implementation, product schema, workflow, or production test exists in the repository; this behavior requires new implementation.

title: Maintain a staged benchmark portfolio
normative_text: The Builder must use one mandatory primary reference harness, contrasting transfer harnesses, and later Visual Asset Editor and Delegation targets before general certification.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Release 1 cannot claim generality beyond its passed reference slice. | Transfer targets are selected for materially different visual, sequencing, runtime, or asset grammars.
repository_coverage_basis: No product implementation exists, and the required algorithm, quality, threshold, or scale behavior must be calibrated with authoritative evidence before production authorization.

title: Maintain a layered versioned benchmark corpus
normative_text: Each benchmark target must contain real specimens, expert goldens, known failures, adversarial near-misses, incomplete or contradictory evidence, controlled mutations, transfer cases, and protected release cases.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Every case has stable IDs, expected behavior, scoring rules, and source provenance. | Protected labels are not exposed to the Builder during ordinary development runs.
repository_coverage_basis: No product implementation exists, and the required algorithm, quality, threshold, or scale behavior must be calibrated with authoritative evidence before production authorization.

title: Generate controlled mutation tests
normative_text: The benchmark system must support one-variable mutations such as preserving topic while changing grammar, preserving grammar while changing topic, removing a sequence invariant, swapping semantic polarity, or injecting irrelevant style evidence.
acceptance_or_enforcement: The expected decision explains which invariant changed. | Mutation cases test causal understanding rather than visual similarity.
repository_coverage_basis: No product implementation exists, and the required algorithm, quality, threshold, or scale behavior must be calibrated with authoritative evidence before production authorization.

title: Protect release-gate benchmark integrity
normative_text: Protected benchmark access, label changes, scoring changes, and case retirement must be governed, versioned, and audited.
acceptance_or_enforcement: A release report discloses any benchmark change since the baseline. | Known answer leakage is a hard release failure.
repository_coverage_basis: No product implementation, product schema, workflow, or production test exists in the repository; this behavior requires new implementation.

title: Score independent quality dimensions
normative_text: Builder evaluation must report evidence understanding, visual and temporal understanding, atomicity, product architecture, skill system, evaluation and repair quality, implementation readiness, and downstream performance as separate dimensions.
acceptance_or_enforcement: The full scorecard remains visible even when a composite trend score is calculated. | Target profiles may define additional category-specific dimensions.
repository_coverage_basis: No product implementation exists, and the required algorithm, quality, threshold, or scale behavior must be calibrated with authoritative evidence before production authorization.

title: Enforce hard release gates
normative_text: Critical unsupported decisions, evidence failures, wrong atomicity, contract contradictions, silent rewrites, untested required skills, benchmark leakage, false readiness, and anti-goal violations must fail release regardless of average score.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Each hard gate maps to a test or review receipt. | A high composite score cannot override a failed hard gate.
repository_coverage_basis: No product implementation, product schema, workflow, or production test exists in the repository; this behavior requires new implementation.

title: Report repeated-run stability
normative_text: Release reports must include distribution and dominant failure patterns for stochastic dimensions rather than a single run score.
acceptance_or_enforcement: Thresholds may include minimum and variance limits in addition to mean score. | A highly variable candidate can fail despite a strong best run.
repository_coverage_basis: No product implementation exists, and the required algorithm, quality, threshold, or scale behavior must be calibrated with authoritative evidence before production authorization.

normative_text: The Builder must classify evidence, visual parse, atomicity, authority, contract, module, skill, capsule, benchmark, budget, provider, observability, migration, and downstream implementation failures with stable codes.

title: Require root-cause investigation before repair

acceptance_or_enforcement: The repair receipt links to the root-cause record. | Repeated guess-and-patch attempts trigger escalation.

title: Compile a Repair and Invalidation Graph
normative_text: For each known failure class, the Builder must define the responsible phase or capability, permitted repair fields, frozen state, invalidated descendants, regression suite, escalation conditions, and authority needed.
acceptance_or_enforcement: A repair cannot edit fields outside its permitted scope. | All declared descendants become stale or invalidated after the repair.

title: Preserve unaffected upstream state

title: Rerun targeted regression suites
normative_text: After repair, the Builder must rerun all tests and benchmarks required by the repaired capability and every affected descendant, including protected cases where policy permits.
acceptance_or_enforcement: A repair is not complete until required regressions pass or remain visibly blocked. | Regression scope is derived from dependency impact rather than manually guessed.

title: Run Index

title: Run Overview

title: Phase, Context, And Dependency Graphs

title: Preflight
normative_text: Before state impact, the UI queries current authority, expected version, affected entities, invalidation impact, required evidence, and whether the command is reversible.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-011;AT-UX-018;AT-UX-019;AT-UX-020;AT-UX-021;AT-UX-022
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.

title: Confirmation
normative_text: Ratify, waive, freeze, authorize, revoke, reject, cancel, rollback, and sensitive export require confirmation with exact scope, consequence, authority, and reason. Typed confirmation is reserved for irreversible or high-impact commands.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-011;AT-UX-018;AT-UX-019;AT-UX-020;AT-UX-021;AT-UX-022;AT-UX-027
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.

title: Submission
normative_text: Commands include idempotency key, expected version, actor identity, reason, evidence refs, and client correlation ID. Controls disable duplicate submission without pretending the command succeeded.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-018;AT-UX-019;AT-UX-020;AT-UX-021;AT-UX-022
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.

title: Resolution
normative_text: Accepted submission shows a pending command receipt. Success appears only after the authoritative domain result is projected. Rejection shows typed reason and unchanged authoritative state.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-018;AT-UX-019;AT-UX-020;AT-UX-021;AT-UX-022;AT-UX-023;AT-UX-024
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.

title: Conflict
normative_text: A stale-version response shows the prior value, current value, intervening receipt, affected command, and deliberate retry path. Automatic overwrite is prohibited.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-009;AT-UX-010;AT-UX-011;AT-UX-018;AT-UX-019;AT-UX-020;AT-UX-021;AT-UX-022
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.

title: Governed export
normative_text: Exports use the same preflight, authority, redaction, receipt, hash, and completion semantics as other governed commands.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-016;AT-UX-018;AT-UX-019;AT-UX-020;AT-UX-021;AT-UX-022;AT-UX-026
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.

title: Loading
normative_text: Preserve final layout dimensions, identify the region loading, and expose cancellation only where the underlying query supports it. Do not display stale values as loading placeholders.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-025
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.

title: Empty
normative_text: Distinguish no records, not configured, not applicable, not yet produced, filtered to zero, and insufficient authority.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-023;AT-UX-024
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.

title: Stale
normative_text: Show projection age, last verified event position, affected regions, and refresh/rebuild status. Hide or disable commands whose preflight cannot prove current state.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-023;AT-UX-024
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.

title: Disconnected
normative_text: Keep the last verified state visibly marked read-only, show disconnection time, queue no privileged commands locally, and resume from the last cursor after reconnect.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-023;AT-UX-024
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.

title: Partial projection
normative_text: Identify missing projector or source, preserve unaffected verified regions, and never calculate a synthetic overall pass.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-023;AT-UX-024
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.

title: Redacted or unauthorized
normative_text: State that information is unavailable without revealing protected content. Distinguish policy redaction from system failure where disclosure permits.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-006;AT-UX-007;AT-UX-008
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.

title: Query or command failure
normative_text: Show typed failure, correlation ID, retained authoritative state, retry eligibility, and escalation route. Raw stack traces and secrets are prohibited.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-018;AT-UX-019;AT-UX-020;AT-UX-021;AT-UX-022
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.

title: Invalidation
normative_text: Mark affected views and artifacts immediately after the invalidating event, retain prior values as historical, and link to invalidation cause and repair graph.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-004;AT-UX-005;AT-UX-014;AT-UX-015;AT-UX-016;AT-UX-019;AT-UX-026;AT-UX-027
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.

title: Query budget
normative_text: Common status and detail views render useful verified content within 2 seconds at reference-corpus scale. Architecture targets p95 API query under 500 ms and event-to-view lag under 2 seconds, subject to empirical calibration.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-017;AT-UX-025
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.

title: Interaction budget
normative_text: Local filter, sort, selection, tab, and inspector interactions target a visible response within 100 ms and completion within 500 ms when no server round trip is required.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-017;AT-UX-025
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.

title: Large collections
normative_text: Tables, event streams, specimens, and graph nodes use bounded pagination, windowing, or server-side queries without losing stable identity, keyboard position, or export completeness.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-017;AT-UX-025
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.

title: UX telemetry
normative_text: Emit web vitals, route/query latency, projection age, command acknowledgement/result latency, render errors, accessibility regressions, disconnections, and export duration correlated to run and command IDs without logging protected content.
acceptance_or_enforcement: AT-UX-001;AT-UX-017;AT-UX-025;AT-UX-027
repository_coverage_basis: Approved UX planning authority; production UI implementation is absent.
